schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The ONE path to durable state, and the owner-gate every write passes through. db_domain is the only module that holds a Postgres connection (MAP.md:334 — the only path to 5432). Its primitives: `connect()` (peer auth over the Unix socket to a dedicated `cairn` database, self-provisioning the database if absent); `create_owned_table(name, owner, columns)` — refuses an ownerless table; `write(table, owner, row)` — refuses any writer but the recorded owner (Law 6); `read(...)`. What it holds is the relational / graph-tree data — the trees a database is uniquely good at. VALIDATIONS were its first consumer but MOVED OUT (2026-07-22) to beside-code git-JSON (cairn/tester/validation_store.py; charter-state-history-split child b): build-provenance is knowledge frozen at PROVED, so it belongs beside the code it explains (Law 5), and git is already durable — no truth record left in one un-backed row.
why: The founding law (MAP.md:367, generalizing UU's button pattern; Law 6): every table has exactly one owner and the owner gates every write; an ownerless table cannot come into existence. This is PHYSICS, not policy (Law 4): the owner registry carries `CHECK (owner <> '')`, so Postgres itself rejects an ownerless row, and `create_owned_table` is the only door — a table that skipped the registry did not come from db_domain, and db_domain is the only module holding a connection. Concentrating durable state behind one gate is also what makes every write instrumentable at one chokepoint (journeys, MAP.md:390) instead of scattered and un-observable.
invoke: import: `from cairn.db_domain import store`. Prove: `python3 cairn/db_domain/proofs/test_db_domain.py` (exit 0 = green). One-time provisioning: an OS-named `LOGIN CREATEDB` role must exist (see `state`).
node_class: device (domain) — a spine device by role (the sole path to 5432). Today it is store primitives; the BaseDevice introspection FACE (intention/state/settings reported live) is a thin wrapper, a filed edge below.
entry: FRESH design, standard mechanism. The design — one owner per table, the owner-gate as a schema CHECK, the registry that owns itself — is Cairn's, tracing to Law 6 / MAP.md:367. (VALIDATIONS were the first consumer; they moved to beside-code git 2026-07-22, leaving db_domain holding only genuinely relational / graph-tree state.) The mechanism is ordinary psycopg2 + peer auth over the Unix socket; nothing crossed as carried code from UU (UU's netpolicy FORWARD-to-Postgres pattern is a LATER stone here, pulled when a sealed build must reach the DB). The connection identity (peer auth, Unix socket, dedicated `cairn` database, vault deferred) was ratified by Akien 2026-07-18.
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) the BaseDevice introspection FACE is not built — the live intention/state/settings surface is a thin wrapper deferred until a prober (tester probe / web UI) pulls it
(b) reads are not owner-gated — Law 6 gates WRITES; a per-consumer read gate is a later need
(c) connect-per-operation, no pool — correctness now, pooling when a real load pulls it
(d) ownership TRANSFER + delegated access (Law 6's 'only through the owner's gate') are recorded-ownership's next capabilities
(e) the FORWARD path (a sealed build reaching this DB over the socket that survives --unshare-net) is proven possible by the seal stone but not yet wired end-to-end
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_db_domain.py, run by the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) create_owned_table produces a table with no recorded owner (ownerless must be impossible — the founding law); (2) a write by a non-owner, or to a table db_domain never created, SUCCEEDS (Law 6 breached); (3) an owned jsonb value comes back stringified rather than as a structured dict (a store that mangles what it holds is worse than none — Law 7); (4) any module OTHER than db_domain opens a connection to 5432 (sole-path breached — enforced by the tester import-scan once that tooth exists).
how_it_learns: Partly, and it is the substrate the rest learn ON. db_domain itself is deliberately dumb physics — it does not adapt; its job is to be the one honest gate, and 'it doesn't learn, because it is the fixed floor others build on' is a valid charter answer. What it ENABLES is the system's durable relational memory: an owner-gated store where the graph trees (and any genuinely relational data a consumer pulls) live as structure, honestly attributed (Law 6) and inspectable. (The system's PROOF memory — 'validated on the 18th', later found expired under Law 3 — is the same idea but moved to beside-code git-JSON, validation_store.py, since provenance is knowledge that belongs beside the code, not a DB row.) Its own horizon: the owner-gate is exactly one owner + write-gating today; ownership transfer, delegated access, and read gates are learned-into as real consumers pull them.
traces_to: Law 6 (exactly one owner; the owner gates every write — the founding DB law, MAP.md:367)
Law 4 (physics over policy — the owner-gate is a schema CHECK, not a convention)
Law 1 (relational state held once as structure, not re-derived)
Law 7 (a store never mangles what it holds — jsonb survives as structure)
MAP.md:334/372 (the only path to 5432; port kernel-closed except via the domain)
build order: db domain, after tester + kernel-owned network (MAP.md:888)
owner: CC owns db_domain's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the founding DB law (one owner per table, MAP.md:367), the connection-identity ratification (peer auth / socket / vault-deferred, 2026-07-18), and the node-class definitions.
proof: proofs/test_db_domain.py — connect reaches `cairn`; ownerless creation refused; owner recorded; the owner gates writes (non-owner + unowned-table refused); an owned jsonb row round-trips as structure. Run by the tester; self-cleaning. Green: `python3 cairn/db_domain/proofs/test_db_domain.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 as the fourth step of the code spine (base -> base pair -> tester -> db_domain), the second real device. Built UNDER the tester's proof gate and dogfooded (the tester's own VALIDATIONS were db_domain's first consumer — later moved to beside-code git 2026-07-22, leaving db_domain the graph-tree/relational store), exactly as Akien directed: 'the tester will validate everything about db_domain as we go.' Advances but does not yet retire CLAUDE.md's 'durable state via store primitives' and 'port 5432 only via db_domain' IOUs — those retire when the tester import-scan makes 'sole path' physics rather than construction.
